questions.question: A worker processes a message then crashes before acknowledging it. Why is this a fundamental problem?
options: The broker can't tell whether the work succeeded, so it must choose between possibly losing the message or possibly redelivering (duplicating) it
questions.explanation: When the ack is lost, the broker genuinely cannot distinguish 'succeeded but ack lost' from 'failed'. Not redelivering risks loss (at-most-once); redelivering risks duplicates (at-least-once). There's no free third option.

questions.question: What does 'at-least-once' delivery mean?
options: Each message delivered exactly one time, guaranteed
Each message delivered one or more times — never lost, but possibly duplicated
Messages may be lost but never duplicated
Messages are delivered only on success
questions.explanation: At-least-once redelivers on uncertainty, so a message is never lost but may arrive more than once. It's the common safe default — combined with idempotent consumers to make the inevitable duplicates harmless.

questions.question: Why is true end-to-end exactly-once DELIVERY effectively impossible?
options: The network can drop the very ack that would resolve the ambiguity, so the sender can never be certain whether to redeliver — any choice risks loss or duplication
questions.explanation: The crash-before-ack ambiguity can't be eliminated, because the message that would resolve it can itself be lost. So exactly-once as a delivery guarantee doesn't exist in general. What's achievable is exactly-once PROCESSING: at-least-once + dedup.

questions.question: Which operation is naturally idempotent (safe to apply multiple times)?
options: POST to create a new record
questions.explanation: Setting an absolute value (SET balance = 100) yields the same result no matter how many times it runs. Incrementing, appending, and creating are NOT idempotent — repeating them changes the result, so they need a dedup key to be made safe under at-least-once delivery.

questions.question: How do you achieve effectively-exactly-once processing under at-least-once delivery?
options: Attach a unique idempotency key to each message and have the consumer atomically skip keys it has already processed
Process every message twice on purpose
questions.explanation: Each message carries a unique idempotency key. The consumer records processed keys in a durable, shared store and skips any key it has seen — atomically (e.g. a DB unique constraint or atomic set). Duplicates are then absorbed harmlessly, giving the effect of exactly-once.
